_note: 现任主帅;换帅经 --set 更新(旧任压进 history,只增不改)。下游:match_header 主帅行。
brief:
- Miroslav Koubek (米罗斯拉夫·库贝克), Czechoslovakia-born coach since 1983, spent decades in lower Czech leagues while working as insurance broker until his 50s before managing Slavia Prague and Viktoria Plzen, winning 2015 league title with latter.

- Appointed Czech national team coach in December after historic Faroe Islands defeat led to Ivan Hasek's sacking, qualified for 2026 World Cup via two penalty shootout wins over Ireland and Denmark within five days, with only 210 minutes of competitive experience before second final.

- Philosophy emphasizes defensive endurance, set-piece mastery (Czechia scored 8 set-piece goals in qualifying, most in Europe), and opportunistic striking; utilizes Coufal's right-flank overlaps and Jurasek's left-side pace in compact, organized structure.

- Inherited settled core of goalkeeper Kovar, midfielder Soucek, and striker Schick, with World Cup opener against South Korea being only his third competitive match, raising concerns about implementing own ideas on squad familiar with previous system.

- At 75, became second-oldest coach at 2026 World Cup after 78-year-old Dick Advocaat of Curaçao, representing late-career recognition after gradual ascent through Czech football.

- Demonstrated squad's character in playoffs; previously promised Viktoria Plzen players a small arm tattoo as 2015 league triumph memento, reflecting player-centric management style.

coach_xverify.discrepancy: Squawka 声称主教练是 Dean Smith（18 小时前发布），但 AP News 有 Koubek 的直接引语，The Guardian 也确认 Koubek 在 Hasek 下课后接任。Squawka 的信息可能过时或错误。
